LLM Knowledge Bases

Something I'm finding very useful recently: using LLMs to build personal knowledge bases for various topics of research interest. In this way, a large fraction of my recent token throughput is going less into manipulating code, and more into manipulating

Oh and in the natural extrapolation, you could imagine that every question to a frontier grade LLM spawns a team of LLMs to automate the whole thing: iteratively construct an entire ephemeral wiki, lint it, loop a few times, then write a full report. Way beyond a `.decode()`.
Gemini was the only frontier model that was susceptible to these sorts of prompt injections (Though we tested Gemini 3 & not 3.1)

The reallocation of resources from sora to tbpn says everything about where content is going...

Wabi CEO Eugenia Kuyda on what makes someone exceptionally effective:

"There's a great quote I think from Ben Horowitz — these 'value-add' VCs that give advice like 'hire A players.'"

"So of course you've got to hire A players, but what does it really mean to be an A player?" https://t.co/9hSIbEJV7W

1) Step 1: always starts in Adobe Firefly Boards 
I use it for brainstorming and generation with AI 

This is my mood-board for this animation https://t.co/JXvvTitKg0

Step 2: 
Framer did first part of the animation, so I needed his sound, I used Premiere Pro to get the soundtrack and remix it to have sound effects of my part 

Just upload your video and export only mp3. Then you can do a lot with that track either in Premiere or in Adobe https://t.co/gcsh5EFMBO

Step 3: 
I use Adobe Express for quick video editing and putting everything together. https://t.co/SjGNCmMESm

Step 4: 
Also I often use Adobe Express to pull frames either to extend animation or to add some multi-frame video inside. It's really easy to save any frame there https://t.co/E5xg89rGtn

Step 5: 
I use upscaler Topaz inside Adobe Firefly Boards to upscale every frame before using those as reference frames for AI video generation https://t.co/kCzdigfHRM
